title: manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type data
text: manydist provides tools constructing dissimilarities numerical, categorical, mixed-type data. main function mdist(). computes dissimilarity object can used distance-based learning workflows, including clustering, nearest-neighbour prediction, diagnostic analyses. package supports preset specifications custom specifications. current interface: method_cat controls categorical dissimilarity; method_num controls preprocessing numerical variables; commensurable controls whether variable-wise contributions placed comparable scale; preset selects predefined distance specification. numerical metric longer user-facing argument. presets, numerical variables compared Manhattan-type contributions. exceptions presets "hl" "euclidean", Euclidean structure part definition. "euclidean" preset replaces former "euclidean_onehot" name. computes Euclidean distances one-hot encoding categorical variables.

title: manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type data
text: use palmerpenguins data illustration. data contain numerical variables, bill length body mass, categorical variables, island sex.

title: manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type data
text: Presets convenient, users can also define custom specifications. custom specification, method_cat controls categorical dissimilarity method_num controls preprocessing applied numerical variables. , categorical variables compared using matching dissimilarities, numerical variables standardized, variable-wise contributions made commensurable. response-aware custom specification can also used. one categorical predictor available, methods require one categorical variable may switch simpler alternative internally.
code: d_custom <- mdist(   penguins_small |> dplyr::select(-species),   preset = "custom",   method_cat = "matching",   method_num = "std",   commensurable = TRUE )  d_custom MDist object   preset : custom   number of observations : 333   number of continuous variables   : 4   number of categorical variables   : 2   parameters:     - categorical method: matching     - numerical preprocessing: std     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE     - number of principal components: 6     - inertia threshold: NULL d_custom_response <- mdist(   penguins_small,   response = species,   preset = "custom",   method_cat = "tvd",   method_num = "std",   commensurable = TRUE )  d_custom_response MDist object   preset : custom   number of observations : 333   number of continuous variables   : 4   number of categorical variables   : 2   parameters:     - categorical method: tvd     - numerical preprocessing: std     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE     - number of principal components: 6     - inertia threshold: NULL

title: manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type data
text: distance representation produced step_mdist() can used downstream supervised learning workflows. illustrate nearest-neighbour classifier using distance--training representation. recipe first converts original mixed-type predictors distances observation training observations. model tuned number neighbours. example uses preset = "gower", workflow can used mdist() preset custom specification based method_cat, method_num, commensurable.
code: library(tidymodels)  set.seed(123)  penguin_split <- rsample::initial_split(   penguins_small,   prop = 0.75,   strata = species )  penguin_train <- rsample::training(penguin_split) penguin_test  <- rsample::testing(penguin_split)  set.seed(123)  penguin_folds <- rsample::vfold_cv(   penguin_train,   v = 5,   strata = species ) rec_knn <- recipes::recipe(species ~ ., data = penguin_train) |>   step_mdist(     recipes::all_predictors(),     preset = "gower",     output = "distance_to_training"   ) knn_spec <- nearest_neighbor_dist(   neighbors = tune::tune() ) |>   parsnip::set_mode("classification") knn_wf <- workflows::workflow() |>   workflows::add_recipe(rec_knn) |>   workflows::add_model(knn_spec)  knn_grid <- tibble::tibble(   neighbors = c(1, 3, 5, 7, 9) )  set.seed(123)  knn_res <- tune::tune_grid(   knn_wf,   resamples = penguin_folds,   grid = knn_grid,   metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy) )  knn_res |>   tune::collect_metrics() # A tibble: 5 × 7   neighbors .metric  .estimator  mean     n std_err .config       <dbl> <chr>    <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr> 1         1 accuracy multiclass 0.996     5 0.00392 pre0_mod1_post0 2         3 accuracy multiclass 1         5 0       pre0_mod2_post0 3         5 accuracy multiclass 0.992     5 0.00485 pre0_mod3_post0 4         7 accuracy multiclass 0.992     5 0.00485 pre0_mod4_post0 5         9 accuracy multiclass 0.988     5 0.00798 pre0_mod5_post0 best_knn <- knn_res |>   tune::select_best(metric = "accuracy")  best_knn # A tibble: 1 × 2   neighbors .config       <dbl> <chr> 1         3 pre0_mod2_post0 final_knn_wf <- knn_wf |>   tune::finalize_workflow(best_knn)  final_knn_fit <- final_knn_wf |>   parsnip::fit(data = penguin_train)  knn_pred <- predict(final_knn_fit, new_data = penguin_test) |>   dplyr::bind_cols(penguin_test |> dplyr::select(species))  yardstick::accuracy(knn_pred, truth = species, estimate = .pred_class) # A tibble: 1 × 3   .metric  .estimator .estimate   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl> 1 accuracy multiclass     0.988

title: Distance-Based Learning for Mixed-Type Data
text: main function manydist mdist(). computes dissimilarities numerical, categorical, mixed-type data. mixed-type dissimilarity can computed preset. example, preset = "gower" computes Gower-type dissimilarity. presets can use response variable. response supplied, used response-aware distance construction treated ordinary predictor. Custom specifications can defined combining categorical method, numerical preprocessing method, commensurability rule.
code: penguins_small <- penguins |>   select(-year) |> drop_na() D <- penguins_small |> select(-species) |>    mdist(preset = "gower")  as.matrix(D$distance)[1:5,1:5] |> round(digits=2) ##      1    2    3    4    5 ## 1 0.00 0.21 0.25 0.24 0.07 ## 2 0.21 0.00 0.07 0.09 0.25 ## 3 0.25 0.07 0.00 0.06 0.26 ## 4 0.24 0.09 0.06 0.00 0.23 ## 5 0.07 0.25 0.26 0.23 0.00 D_resp <- penguins_small |> mdist(   response = species,   preset = "u_dep" )  D_resp ## MDist object ##   preset : u_dep  ##   number of observations : 333  ##   number of continuous variables   : 4  ##   number of categorical variables   : 2  ##   parameters: ##     - categorical method: tvd ##     - numerical preprocessing: pc_scores ##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE D_custom <- mdist(penguins_small |> select(-species),                   preset = "custom",                   method_cat = "matching",                   method_num = "std",                   commensurable = TRUE )  D_custom ## MDist object ##   preset : custom  ##   number of observations : 333  ##   number of continuous variables   : 4  ##   number of categorical variables   : 2  ##   parameters: ##     - categorical method: matching ##     - numerical preprocessing: std ##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE ##     - number of principal components: 6 ##     - inertia threshold: NULL

code: benchmark_mdist(x, response = NULL, specs = all_dist_method_specs())

title: Benchmark `mdist()` over multiple method specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: x data frame tibble predictors, optionally including response column. response Optional response column inside `x`, supplied either unquoted character string. specs tibble method specifications. default, generated [all_dist_method_specs()]. must contain columns `spec_type`, `preset`, `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`.

title: Benchmark `mdist()` over multiple method specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: tibble containing supplied specifications together : result corresponding output [mdist()], error object   specification failed. ok Logical indicator; `TRUE` run completed successfully,   `FALSE` otherwise. error Error message failed runs, `NA` otherwise.

title: Benchmark `mdist()` over multiple method specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: row `specs` interpreted one valid `mdist()` configuration. Preset-based custom component-based specifications supported. Failed specifications caught returned output rather stopping full benchmark. Preset specifications use `preset` column ignore `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`. Component specifications evaluated `preset = "custom"` use `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`. function intended benchmarking, validation, sensitivity analyses across multiple distance specifications.

title: Benchmark `mdist()` over multiple method specifications — benchmark_mdist

code: compare_lovo_mdist(   x,   methods,   dims = 2,   keep_dist = FALSE,   .progress = FALSE,   ... )

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
text: x data frame tibble containing predictors. methods **named list** describing distance specifications compare. element must list arguments passed lovo_mdist. example: dims Number dimensions used MDS configuration computing congruence-based diagnostics. keep_dist Logical; TRUE, distance matrices LOVO computations retained. increases memory usage. .progress Logical; TRUE, progress messages printed computing LOVO diagnostics method. ... Additional arguments passed lovo_mdist unless overridden method-specific argument list. may include optional clustering diagnostics, example cluster_k, cluster_methods, hclust_method.
code: methods = list(   gower = list(preset = "gower"),   u_dep = list(preset = "unbiased_dependent"),   custom = list(     preset = "custom",     method_cat = "matching",     method_num = "std",     commensurable = TRUE   ) )

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
text: object class MDistLOVOCompare containing: results tibble one row per method-variable combination. methods list distance specifications used. dims Number MDS dimensions used. n_obs Number observations dataset.

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
text: distance specification, function: Computes full mixed-type distance using mdist(). Recomputes distance repeatedly leaving one variable time. Measures impact variable using metrics mean absolute deviation (MAD), congruence-based diagnostics, , requested, clustering-based agreement measures. results combined across methods returned MDistLOVOCompare object, supports print(), summary(), ggplot2::autoplot().

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(manydist) library(palmerpenguins)  data <- penguins |>   dplyr::select(-species) |>   tidyr::drop_na()  cmp <- compare_lovo_mdist(   x = data,   methods = list(     gower = list(preset = "gower"),     u_dep = list(preset = "unbiased_dependent")   ),   cluster_k = 3 )  summary(cmp) autoplot(cmp, metric = "mad_importance") autoplot(cmp, metric = "pam_importance") } # }

what: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: Computes congruence coefficient two data configurations using Frobenius inner product pairwise distance matrices.

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
code: congruence_coeff(L1, L2)

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: L1 numeric matrix data frame (rows = observations) L2 numeric matrix data frame number rows L1

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: scalar \([-1,1]\) measuring similarity two configurations.

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: congruence coefficient defined $$   \frac{\langle D_1, D_2 \rangle_F}        {\sqrt{\langle D_1, D_1 \rangle_F \langle D_2, D_2 \rangle_F}} $$ \(D_1\) \(D_2\) pairwise distance matrices derived L1 L2.

title: Leave-one-variable-out diagnostics for distance-based variable importance — lovo_mdist
text: `lovo_mdist()` useful assessing strongly predictor contributes distance-based representation. predictor considered influential removing produces large change dissimilarity matrix, multidimensional scaling configuration, optional clustering partition. returned object contains several LOVO diagnostics. main distance contribution measured mean absolute difference full dissimilarity matrix leave-one-variable-matrix (`mad_importance`). normalized version stored `relative_distance`. function also compares classical multidimensional scaling configurations computed full leave-one-variable-dissimilarities. diagnostics stored `mds_congruence` / `cc_importance` `ac_importance`, latter corresponding alienation coefficient. `cluster_k` supplied, function additionally computes clustering partitions full leave-one-variable-dissimilarities compares using adjusted Rand index. corresponding importance measures defined `1 - ARI` stored `pam_importance`, `hclust_importance`, `spectral_importance`, depending selected clustering methods. Clustering-based diagnostics require suggested package `mclust`.

Columns can numeric, factors, mixture . new_data Optional data frame matrix containing new observations. supplied, distances computed rows `new_data` rows `x`, producing rectangular test--training dissimilarity matrix. response Optional response variable used response-aware categorical dissimilarities. can supplied unquoted column name character string. response column removed predictors computing distances. method_cat Character string specifying categorical-variable dissimilarity used `preset = "custom"`. Common values include `"matching"` `"tvd"`. Use [all_dist_method_specs()] inspect available methods. method_num Character string specifying numerical-variable preprocessing used `preset = "custom"`. Available options include `"none"` preprocessing, `"std"` standard-deviation scaling, `"range"` range scaling, `"robust"` inter-quartile-range-based scaling, `"pc_scores"` principal-component score scaling. commensurable Logical. `TRUE`, dissimilarities scaled average contribution variable overall distance equal 1. ncomp Integer `NULL`. Number principal components retain `method_num = "pc_scores"`. `NULL`, available components used unless `threshold` supplied supported underlying method. threshold Numeric `NULL`. Optional cumulative variance threshold used `method_num = "pc_scores"`. preset Character string specifying predefined distance specification. Available values include `"custom"`, `"gower"`, `"unbiased_dependent"`, `"u_dep"`, `"u_indep"`, `"u_mix"`, `"hl"`, `"gudmm"`, `"dkss"`, `"mod_gower"`, `"euclidean"`. `preset` `"custom"`, arguments `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`, `interaction` handled preset user-supplied values arguments ignored. interaction Logical. `TRUE`, adds interaction-aware continuous-categorical component based local predictive separability. prop_nn Numeric. Proportion nearest neighbours used `interaction = TRUE`. score Character string specifying score used `interaction = TRUE`. Available values include `"ba"` balanced accuracy `"logloss"`. decision Character string specifying decision rule used `score = "ba"`. default `"prior_corrected"`. gower_average Logical; used `preset = "gower"`. `TRUE`, returns standard Gower dissimilarity averaged variables, matching scale [cluster::daisy()] `metric = "gower"`. `FALSE`, returns sum per-variable Gower contributions, equivalent multiplying averaged Gower dissimilarity number active variables.

title: Mixed-type dissimilarities for distance-based learning — mdist
text: object class `"MDist"`. object contains computed   dissimilarity `$distance` field, selected `preset`, training   data, list parameters describing fitted distance   specification. Square train-train dissimilarities stored   `"dissimilarity"`/`"dist"` objects; rectangular test--training   dissimilarities stored `"dissimilarity"`/`"matrix"` objects.

title: Mixed-type dissimilarities for distance-based learning — mdist
text: `mdist()` main distance-construction function `manydist`. can return ordinary train-train dissimilarities rectangular test--training dissimilarities `new_data` supplied. resulting object stores dissimilarity matrix metadata distance specification used. `preset = "custom"`, users manually choose numerical preprocessing, categorical dissimilarity, commensurability, optional interaction term. `"gower"` preset follows usual Gower construction based range scaling continuous variables matching dissimilarities categorical variables. `gower_average` argument controls whether result averaged variables returned sum variable-wise contributions. `"u_dep"`, `"unbiased_dependent"`, `"u_indep"`, `"u_mix"` presets convenience specifications unbiased commensurable mixed-variable dissimilarities. `"euclidean"` preset computes Euclidean distance one-hot encoding categorical variables. `"gudmm"`, `"dkss"`, `"mod_gower"` presets provide additional mixed-type distance constructions. presets currently support train-train distances stop `new_data` supplied. Use [all_dist_method_specs()] inspect available distance components method specifications.

code: if (requireNamespace("palmerpenguins", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("penguins", package = "palmerpenguins")    penguins_small <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>     dplyr::select(       bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm, flipper_length_mm,       body_mass_g, species, island, sex     ) |>     tidyr::drop_na()    # Gower distance on mixed-type data   d_gower <- mdist(penguins_small, preset = "gower")   d_gower    # Custom mixed-type specification   d_custom <- mdist(     penguins_small,     preset = "custom",     method_cat = "matching",     method_num = "std",     commensurable = TRUE   )    d_custom    # Train-to-new-data distances   penguin_split <- rsample::initial_split(penguins_small, prop = 0.75)   penguin_train <- rsample::training(penguin_split)   penguin_test  <- rsample::testing(penguin_split)    d_new <- mdist(     penguin_train,     new_data = penguin_test,     preset = "gower"   )    d_new } #> MDist object #>   preset : gower  #>   number of training observations : 249  #>   number of test observations     : 84  #>   number of continuous variables   : 4  #>   number of categorical variables   : 3  #>   parameters: #>     - commensurability adjustment: FALSE

text: step can produce either distances new observations training observations, within-training pairwise dissimilarity matrix. former usual choice supervised prediction workflows; latter useful distance-based clustering workflows fitted training data. [recipes::prep()], `step_mdist()` stores selected training predictors fits internal manydist preprocessor. [recipes::bake()], selected predictors removed replaced distance columns named `dist_1`, `dist_2`, . `output = "distance_to_training"`, baking training data returns training pairwise distances, baking new data returns distances new observation training observation. rectangular representation suitable nearest-neighbour prediction models. `output = "pairwise"`, step returns within-training pairwise dissimilarity matrix. Baking genuinely new data supported mode, output intended training-clustering workflows [pam_dist()] [spectral_dist()].

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-09

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Expanded manydist package focused mixed-type distance construction broader framework distance-based learning mixed-type data. Updated package title description reflect support distance construction, distance-based modelling workflows, variable-importance diagnostics, clustering. Changed package maintainer Angelos Markos Alfonso Iodice D’Enza.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added revised mdist() interface documentation mixed-type distance construction. Added support additional mixed-type distance specifications presets. Added response-aware distance construction tools supervised mixed-type workflows. Added interaction-aware distance components continuous-categorical relationships. Added helper infrastructure preprocessing applying mixed-type distance specifications consistently across training new data. Added utilities generating benchmarking distance-method specifications.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added step_mdist() integrating manydist distances recipes tidymodels workflows. Added nearest_neighbor_dist() related prediction functions nearest-neighbour models based precomputed manydist-generated distances. Added pam_dist() partitioning around medoids using manydist dissimilarities. Added spectral_dist() spectral_from_dist() spectral clustering distance matrices. Added support functions converting distances affinities fitting distance-based clustering models.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added lovo_mdist() leave-one-variable-diagnostics distance matrices. Added compare_lovo_mdist() lovo_method_spec() comparing LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications. Added congruence- alienation-based diagnostics comparing multidimensional scaling configurations. Added optional clustering-based LOVO diagnostics using PAM, hierarchical clustering, spectral clustering.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added gen_mixed() generate_dataset() generating mixed-type example simulation data. Added benchmark_mdist() benchmarking distance specifications across datasets method grids. Added all_dist_method_specs() distance-method metadata helpers.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added documentation new modelling, clustering, LOVO, benchmarking, recipe functions. Updated package-level description metadata 0.5.0 release. Removed CRAN-inappropriate development files, caches, vignette outputs source build.
